current.title: Action-Doing Mode Probe
current.description: Tests MECH-025: cognitive_modes.action_doing_mode. Asserts that the system enters a distinct action-doing mode when executing intentions with causal consequences, measurable as an E3 precision lift on agent_caused_hazard steps vs baseline. HIGH_CAUSAL (num_hazards=8) vs LOW_CAUSAL (num_hazards=1). Pass requires: HIGH precision lift > LOW lift + 0.05 AND HIGH harm_reduction_slope > LOW + 0.05.

queue.title: Action-Doing Mode Probe
queue.description: Tests MECH-025: cognitive_modes.action_doing_mode. Asserts that the system enters a distinct action-doing mode when executing intentions with causal consequences, measurable as an E3 precision lift on agent_caused_hazard steps vs baseline. HIGH_CAUSAL (num_hazards=8) vs LOW_CAUSAL (num_hazards=1). Pass requires: HIGH precision lift > LOW lift + 0.05 AND HIGH harm_reduction_slope > LOW + 0.05.

completed.title: Write-Locus Contamination (MECH-060 / MECH-067)
completed.description: Tests whether write-locus separation between pre-commit (sim_error) and post-commit (realized_error) channels is load-bearing for attribution reliability. Three conditions: FULL, CONTAMINATED_DURABLE, CONTAMINATED_RESIDUE.
completed.result_summary: FULL last-Q harm: 0.929 | CONT_DURABLE last-Q harm: 0.915 | CONT_RESIDUE last-Q harm: 0.593 | residue inflation: 8520x | MECH-060/067 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Control Plane Precision Separation
completed.description: Tests whether E3 score dispersion (confidence proxy) and E1 prediction error are independent signals, and whether routing them separately reduces harm.
completed.result_summary: |corr(dispersion, PE)| = 0.067 (<0.30 threshold) — signals independent | SEPARATED last-Q harm 0.663 vs MERGED 0.875 — separation beneficial

completed.title: Residue Trajectory Placement
completed.description: Tests whether residue accumulates at trajectory positions during rollout, not only at the final executed step. Validates REE invariant that harm sites are precisely localised along the planned path rather than post-hoc.
completed.result_summary: TRAJECTORY-WIDE  last-Q harm: 0.724 | ENDPOINT-ONLY    last-Q harm: 0.813 | MECH-056 verdict: PASS

completed.title: E1/E2 Timescale Ablation
completed.description: Ablates the E1 (LSTM slow predictor) versus E2 (fast transition predictor, cerebellum-like) timescale separation. Tests whether the two-speed architecture improves latent stability compared to a single-speed baseline on ree-v1-minimal.
completed.result_summary: SAME-TIMESCALE last-Q harm: 0.887 | Performance criterion (harm <=)?   YES | MECH-058 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: Commitment Boundary Token Reclassification
completed.description: Validates that REE's commitment boundary correctly reclassifies pre-commit simulation errors as post-commit realised errors. Tests the boundary token approach retained under the retain_ree adjudication.
completed.result_summary: Distinct signals (|corr| < 0.7)?   YES  (0.114) | MECH-061 verdict: PASS | Pre-commit (E2) predictions and post-commit (env) harm signals carry

completed.title: Control Completion Requirement
completed.description: Tests MECH-057 (control_completion_requirement): whether REE's control plane requires action completion before new precision updates are admitted. Needs design review following JEPA decoupling.
completed.result_summary: NO_ATTRIBUTION last-Q harm: 0.896  (↑ 1.9%) | NO_GATING      last-Q harm: 0.919  (↑ 4.5%) | MECH-057 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: E1/E2 Associative vs Transition Knowledge (MultiRoomGrid)
completed.description: Tests the orthogonality of E1 (associative engine, register of what IS) vs E2 (transition model, how things change) using a multi-room environment where quadrant hazard density creates room-type associations (E1 territory) orthogonal to action-transition physics (E2 territory). Four conditions: FULL, E1_FROZEN, E2_FROZEN, SAME_RATE. PASS: FULL < both frozen conditions AND <= SAME_RATE*1.10.
completed.result_summary: E2_FROZEN  last-Q harm: 1.1100 | SAME_RATE  last-Q harm: 1.1100 | MECH-058 / EXQ-006 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: Multi-Step Control Completion Gating
completed.description: Provides the richer MECH-057 substrate that EXQ-004 lacked: macro-actions spanning MACRO_LENGTH=4 sub-steps. Tests whether suppressing precision updates during committed macro-action execution (GATED) reduces harm vs mid-sequence disruption (UNGATED) and atomic-action baseline (NO_MACRO).
completed.result_summary: UNGATED  last-Q harm: 0.7720 | NO_MACRO last-Q harm: 0.5287 | MECH-057 / EXQ-007 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: E3 Candidate Count Ablation
completed.description: Tests whether the number of E2 trajectory candidates available to E3 is load-bearing for harm avoidance. Three conditions: SINGLE (1 candidate, no real selection), STANDARD (32, default), EXPANDED (128, wider search). PASS: STANDARD < SINGLE (selection matters) AND EXPANDED <= STANDARD*1.10 (no collapse at high counts).
completed.result_summary: STANDARD (n=32)  last-Q harm: 0.854 | EXPANDED (n=128) last-Q harm: 0.836 | MECH-063 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Residue Routing Weight Sensitivity
completed.description: Tests whether the E3 residue routing weight (rho_residue) is load-bearing. Four conditions: RHO_ZERO (rho=0, agent is ethically amnesic), RHO_LOW (0.1), RHO_STANDARD (0.5, default), RHO_HIGH (2.0). PASS: RHO_STANDARD < RHO_ZERO (residue routing helps) AND RHO_HIGH <= RHO_ZERO*1.20 (high weight doesn't catastrophically destabilise).
completed.result_summary: RHO_HIGH     (rho=2.0) last-Q harm: 0.749 | MECH-062 verdict: PASS | (RHO_ZERO) degrades harm avoidance vs the standard setting,

completed.title: Control Plane Precision Separation (extended seeds)
completed.description: Extended-seed re-run of EXQ-000 (MECH-059 PASS). Adds seeds 13, 77, 123, 256, 314, 999 for tighter confidence intervals. Same MERGED vs SEPARATED conditions. Contributes to P1 evidence accumulation for the precision channel separation claim.
completed.result_summary: Signals independent (corr < 0.3)?  YES  (0.045) | Separation neutral or beneficial?   YES | MECH-059 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Residue Trajectory Placement (extended seeds)
completed.description: Extended-seed re-run of EXQ-001 (MECH-056 PASS). Adds seeds 13, 77, 123, 256, 314, 999. TRAJECTORY-WIDE vs ENDPOINT-ONLY conditions. Builds replication depth for the residue placement claim.
completed.result_summary: TRAJECTORY-WIDE  last-Q harm: 0.804 | ENDPOINT-ONLY    last-Q harm: 0.936 | MECH-056 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Commitment Boundary Token Reclassification (extended seeds)
completed.description: Extended-seed re-run of EXQ-003 (MECH-061 PASS). Adds seeds 13, 77, 123, 256, 314, 999. PRE_COMMIT vs POST_COMMIT channel correlation conditions. Tightens confidence on commit boundary separation.
completed.result_summary: Distinct signals (|corr| < 0.7)?   YES  (0.214) | MECH-061 verdict: PASS | Pre-commit (E2) predictions and post-commit (env) harm signals carry

completed.title: Write-Locus Contamination (extended seeds)
completed.description: Extended-seed re-run of EXQ-005 (MECH-060 PASS). Adds seeds 13, 77, 123, 256, 314, 999. FULL vs CONTAMINATED_DURABLE vs CONTAMINATED_RESIDUE. Builds replication depth on the write-locus separation result.
completed.result_summary: Criterion 2 (harm ordering):      YES  (full=0.836 <= max_cont=0.873*1.05=0.916?) | MECH-060/067 verdict: PASS | as good for harm reduction as contaminated conditions.

completed.title: Control Plane Precision Separation — V2 Parity
completed.description: Parity check #1. Replicates V1 EXQ-013 PASS result on REE-v2 substrate (CausalGridWorld). Tests whether separating slow-timescale E1 precision updates from fast-timescale E3 policy decisions reduces hazard collision rates compared to a merged control plane. Closes SD-001 parity gap for MECH-059.
completed.result_summary: Signals independent (corr < 0.3)?  YES  (0.122) | Separation neutral or beneficial?   YES | MECH-059 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Residue Trajectory Placement — V2 Parity
completed.description: Parity check #2. Replicates V1 PASS result for MECH-056 on REE-v2 substrate. Tests whether residue accumulates at trajectory positions during rollout (trajectory-wide placement) vs only the final step (endpoint-only). Validates REE invariant that harm sites are precisely localised along the planned path.
completed.result_summary: TRAJECTORY-WIDE  last-Q harm: 1.249 | ENDPOINT-ONLY    last-Q harm: 1.227 | MECH-056 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Write Locus Contamination — V2 Parity
completed.description: Parity check #3. Replicates V1 PASS result for MECH-060/MECH-067 on REE-v2 substrate. Tests whether contamination is properly attributed to write loci vs residual signal bleed. Three conditions: FULL, CONTAMINATED_DURABLE, CONTAMINATED_RESIDUE. CausalGridWorld's contamination footprint makes this directly testable.
completed.result_summary: CONT_RESIDUE   last-Q harm: 1.2087  |attr_corr|: 0.1591  residue: 2535.5296 | Criterion 2 (harm ordering):      YES  (full=1.245 <= max_cont=1.224*1.05?) | MECH-060/067 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Commitment Boundary Validation — V2 Parity
completed.description: Parity check #4. Replicates V1 PASS result for MECH-061 on REE-v2 substrate. Tests whether the commit boundary correctly separates pre-commit simulation errors (E2 harm predictions) from post-commit realised errors (actual env harm), and whether keeping them separate is at least as good as blending them into a single signal.
completed.result_summary: |pre_post_corr| WITH-BOUNDARY (mean): 0.072 | Distinct signals (|corr| < 0.7)?   YES  (0.072) | MECH-061 verdict: PASS

completed.title: CausalGridWorld Baseline — Step 2.3 Environment Validation
completed.description: Step 2.3 exit criterion validation. New V2 experiment (no V1 equivalent). Tests that CausalGridWorld produces structurally distinct agent_caused_hazard vs env_caused_hazard transitions, that the baseline REE agent creates a non-trivial contamination footprint, and that agent causal contribution exceeds the 10% threshold. PASS confirms SD-003 substrate is ready.
completed.result_summary: agent_caused_harm_count_total: 343 | env_caused_harm_count_total:   96 | SD-003 substrate verdict: PASS  (4/4 criteria met)

completed.title: E1/E2 Terrain-Timescale Separation — V2 Redesign
completed.description: Ground-up redesign for MECH-058 on REE-v2 substrate. Exploits CausalGridWorld's two natural timescales: slow contamination terrain (E1 domain) vs fast env drift every 5 steps (policy domain). Three conditions: SEPARATED (e1_lr=5e-5, policy_lr=1e-3), COLLAPSED_FAST (both 1e-3), COLLAPSED_SLOW (both 5e-5). New metrics: contamination_avoidance_rate and post_drift_harm_rate directly test whether matched timescale separation yields functional benefit. Addresses V1 failure root cause: V1 GridWorld gave both LRs identical convergence terrain.
completed.result_summary: Crit 2  drift harm SEP=0.123  CS=0.116  delta=+0.008  (need <-0.02)  MISSED | Crit 3  harm       SEP=1.232  <= 1.238*1.1=1.362  MET | Criteria met: 1/3  ->  MECH-058 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: Attribution Completion Gating — V2 Redesign
completed.description: Ground-up redesign for MECH-057 on REE-v2 substrate. Uses CausalGridWorld's info['transition_type'] == 'agent_caused_hazard' as a concrete self-attribution completion signal. Three conditions gate the REINFORCE return signal differently: COMPLETION_GATED (G = -agent_caused_harm only), FREE_REPLAN (G = -total_harm), ATTRIBUTION_BLIND (G = -env_caused_harm only). Tests whether gating the policy update to completed self-attribution events reduces agent-caused harm vs both mixed and attribution-absent baselines. Addresses V1 failure root cause: V1 GridWorld had no 'action in progress' state for gating to exploit.
completed.result_summary: FREE_REPLAN       agent_harm (last-Q): 0.864 | ATTRIBUTION_BLIND agent_harm (last-Q): 0.819 | Criteria met: 1/3  ->  MECH-057 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: Rollout Viability Mapping
completed.description: Tests ARC-018: hippocampus.rollout_viability_mapping. Asserts that E1 world-model updating from prediction-reality mismatch builds an internal viability map of trajectory sequences. VIABILITY_MAPPED condition (E1 updates every step) vs VIABILITY_FIXED (E1 frozen; policy gradient still runs). Pass requires: MAPPED slope > FIXED + 0.05 AND MAPPED last-Q harm < FIXED * 0.95.
completed.result_summary: VIABILITY_FIXED  harm (last-Q): 1.231  slope=+0.009 | Crit 2  harm  MAPPED=1.254 < FIXED*0.95=1.170  MISSED | Criteria met: 0/2  ->  ARC-018 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: Valence-Regime Correlation
completed.description: Tests Q-007: emotion.universal_expression_channel_mapping. Asserts that internal valence (health) correlates with E3 precision modulation across resource regimes. RESOURCE_RICH (num_resources=10) vs RESOURCE_SPARSE (num_resources=1). Pass requires: RICH Pearson corr(health, precision) > 0.10 AND RICH mean_precision > SPARSE + 0.10.
completed.result_summary: Crit 1  RICH corr=+0.000 > 0.1  MISSED | Criteria met: 0/2  ->  Q-007 verdict: FAIL | Results saved to: /Users/dgolden/Documents/GitHub/REE_Working/ree-v2/evidence/experiments/valence_regime_correlation/valence_regime_correlation_20260315T165926.json

completed.title: Kernel Chaining Interface
completed.description: Tests MECH-033: hippocampus.kernel_chaining_interface. Asserts that E2 supplies short-horizon forward-prediction kernels and hippocampal systems chain these into explicit multi-step rollouts. WITH_CHAIN (generate_trajectories + E3 select) vs NO_CHAIN (random action; E1 still learns). Pass requires: WITH_CHAIN last-Q harm < NO_CHAIN * 0.90 AND WITH_CHAIN slope > NO_CHAIN slope + 0.05.
completed.result_summary: WITH_CHAIN harm (last-Q): 1.230  slope=+0.003 | NO_CHAIN   harm (last-Q): 1.253  slope=+0.013 | Criteria met: 0/2  ->  MECH-033 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: E2 Attribution Calibration
completed.description: Tests MECH-071: does trained E2.predict_harm(z_t, a_actual) calibrate higher before agent_caused_hazard transitions than env_caused_hazard transitions? Two conditions: TRAINED (200 warmup episodes) vs RANDOM (untrained E2 control). Pass: TRAINED calibration_gap > 0.05 AND |RANDOM gap| < 0.10. Prerequisite for EXQ-028 (selective residue attribution).
completed.result_summary: MECH-071 / EXQ-027 verdict: FAIL | E2 cannot reliably distinguish agent-caused from env-caused harm.

completed.title: Selective Residue Attribution
completed.description: Tests MECH-072: does gating residue accumulation on E2 harm foreseeability (predict_harm > 0.3) reduce false attribution rate vs naive accumulation? Three conditions: NAIVE (all harm), FORESEEABLE (E2-gated), ORACLE (ground truth). Pass: false_attribution_rate[FORESEEABLE] < false_attribution_rate[NAIVE] AND final_harm[FORESEEABLE] <= final_harm[NAIVE] * 1.05. ORACLE provides interpretive upper bound.
completed.result_summary: Criterion 2 (FORE harm <= NAIVE*1.05)?  YES  (1.2396 <= 1.3016) | ORACLE interpretation: false_rate=0.0000  harm=1.2289 | MECH-072 / EXQ-028 verdict: FAIL

completed.title: Path Memory Ablation
completed.description: Tests ARC-007: hippocampus.path_memory. Asserts that access to prior trajectory information (contamination_view + footprint_density in observation) is load-bearing for contamination avoidance. PATH_MEMORY (full obs) vs PATH_ABLATED (path-memory slice zeroed). Pass requires: MEMORY agent_caused_harm < ABLATED * 0.95 AND MEMORY total_harm <= ABLATED * 1.05.
completed.result_summary: PATH_MEMORY  agent_harm (last-Q): 0.875  total: 1.228 | PATH_ABLATED agent_harm (last-Q): 0.941  total: 1.231 | Criteria met: 2/2  ->  ARC-007 verdict: PASS

completed.title: Precision Regime Probe
completed.description: Tests ARC-016: cognitive_modes.control_plane_regimes. Asserts that distinct E3 precision regimes produce distinct behavioral signatures. HIGH_REGIME (precision_max=2.0, min=1.0) vs LOW_REGIME (precision_max=0.5, min=0.1). Pass requires: |HIGH-LOW| mean precision > 0.30 AND (HIGH commit_rate < LOW - 0.05 OR HIGH last-Q harm < LOW * 0.95).
completed.result_summary: LOW_REGIME   precision=0.300  commit=0.000  harm=1.247 | Crit 2  commit 1.000<-0.050? N  OR  harm 1.239<1.184? N  MISSED | Criteria met: 1/2  ->  ARC-016 verdict: FAIL
